zh.lifeSummary: 當Wiliam Mainwaring Mering於大約1345年出生在英國爾奧維 奧福時，他的父親Alexander de Mering21歲，母親Maud de Hercy18歲。他於1380年與Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering x在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1399年之後在英國英格蘭爾奧維，埋葬在英國爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Wiliam Mainwaring Mering于大约1345年出生在英国爾奧維 奧福时，他的父亲Alexander de Mering21岁，母亲Maud de Hercy18岁。他于1380年与Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering x在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1399年之后在英国英格兰爾奧維，埋葬在英国爾。

nameMeanings.name: Mering
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hasso, Alfons, Alois, Arno, Ernst, Erwin, Florian, Gerhard, Guenther, Kurt, Ludwig, Manfred.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German hærinc ‘herring’, German Hering, a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, particularly a seller of herrings, or a nickname for someone supposedly resembling a herring. In some cases the Jewish surname is artificial. In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the German and Jewish variant Häring. Compare Haring .

German and Dutch: habitational name from any of several places and farmhouses so named, for instance near Zeesse in Overijssel, originally inhabited by a certain Here or Hero and his kin. Here or Hero is a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name with the element heri ‘army’.

variants.name: Haring
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (mainly Häring); Dutch: from Middle Low German hārinc ‘herring’, German Hering, a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, particularly a seller of herringss, or a nickname for someone supposedly resembling a herring. In some cases the Jewish surname is artificial. Compare Haering and Hering .
Austrian German: nickname for a skinny person (like a herring; see 1 above).
German (Häring): habitational name from Häring (Bavaria, Tyrol) or Häringen (Baden-Württemberg).

variants.name: Herink
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Reiner.
Dutch: variant of   Hering   2.

variants.name: Herring
meanings: English (London), Dutch, and German: metonymic occupational name for a herring fisher or for a seller of the fish, or perhaps for someone who habitually ate herring, from Middle English hering (Old English hǣring, hēring), Dutch haring, Middle High German hærinc. In some cases it may have been a nickname in the sense of a trifle, something of little value, a meaning which is found in medieval phrases and proverbial expressions such as ‘to like neither herring nor barrel’, i.e. not to like something at all.
German: habitational name from Herringen in Westphalia.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Hering  .

variants.name: Heuring
meanings: German: variant of Höring, probably a vowel variant of Hering .

variants.name: Hoerig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Kurt.
German (Hörig): variant of Hering .

German (Hörig): nickname from Middle High German hœrec ‘obedient, in bondage’, Middle Low German horich ‘obedient, belonging to’. In some cases from Middle Low German horink ‘child born out of wedlock’.

nameMeanings.name: Wiliam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Mainwaring
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Mesnil-Varin in Saint-Paër (Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie). The placename means ‘domain of Warin’ (see   Waring  ). Compare   Manwaring   and   Mandry  .
Irish: adopted for Ó Manaráin, see Mannering 2.

variants.name: Manwaring
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Mainwaring  .

variants.name: Waring
meanings: An old name. The same as Waring or Guerin, who came over with the Conqueror; a name probably connected with that of the Waernas or Wernas. the Varini or Verini, a people of the Elbe

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Wiliam Mainwaring Mering was born about 1345, in Over Peover, Cheshire, England, his father, Alexander de Mering, was 21 and his mother, Maud de Hercy, was 18. He married Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering x in 1380, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died after 1399, in Over, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Wiliam Mainwaring Mering est né environ 1345, à Over Peover, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Alexander de Mering, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Maud de Hercy, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering x en 1380, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé après 1399, à Over, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterré à Cheshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Wiliam Mainwaring Mering um 1345 in Over Peover, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Alexander de Mering 21 und seine Mutter Maud de Hercy 18. Er hat 1380 in England, United Kingdom Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering x geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Er starb nach 1399 in Over, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Cheshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Wiliam Mainwaring Mering è nato il circa 1345 nel Over Peover, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Alexander de Mering, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Maud de Hercy, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering x nel 1380, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì dopo il 1399, nel Over, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolto a Cheshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Wiliam Mainwaring Meringが約1345年にイギリスチェシュアイアオバー ペオバーで生まれた時，父Alexander de Meringは21歳，母Maud de Hercyは18歳であった。1380年にイギリスイギリスでLady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering xと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1399年以後にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアオバーで死去し，イギリスチェシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Wiliam Mainwaring Mering(이)가 대략 1345년 영국 체실르 버 p오버에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alexander de Mering(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud de Hercy(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1380년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering x(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1399년 이후 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 버에서 사망했으며 영국 체실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Wiliam Mainwaring Mering nasceu aproximadamente 1345, em Over Peover, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Alexander de Mering, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Hercy, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering x em 1380, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu após 1399, em Over, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultado em Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Wiliam Mainwaring Mering родился около 1345 г. в местности овир пеовир, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Alexander de Mering было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Maud de Hercy, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering x в 1380 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер после 1399 г. в местности овир, чешир, Англия, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности чешир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Wiliam Mainwaring Mering nació aproximadamente 1345, en Over Peover, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Alexander de Mering, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Maud de Hercy, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Lady Elizabeth Millicent Beckering x en 1380, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió después de 1399, en Over, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultado en Cheshire, Inglaterra.
